name: Dorset Maritime

geology_and_soil.organic_matter: Low-moderate (plantation history, limited leaf litter cycling)

topography.aspect: Mixed — N-facing combes, S-facing slopes, ridgelines
topography.streams: Several small streams draining to River Hooke

ecology.protected_species: Hazel dormouse, multiple bat species, great crested newt (adjacent)
ecology.designation: None formal — but adjacent to Dorset AONB, Powerstock Common SSSI nearby

management_history.pre_1950: Mixed broadleaf woodland, some ancient origins
management_history.1950_1990: Extensive conifer planting (Scots pine, Douglas fir, larch) for timber
management_history.1990_present: AA School (Hooke Park campus) — experimental timber, some broadleaf restoration
management_history.current_state: Mosaic: plantation conifer (thinned), regenerating broadleaf, managed oak, experimental plots
management_history.key_challenge: Transition from production conifer to resilient mixed woodland under climate change

management_objectives: Phase conifer out over 100 years (financially constrained)
Establish resilient mixed broadleaf under climate adaptation
Create habitat connectivity (corridors, riparian buffers, deadwood)
Maintain timber revenue (high-value hardwood long rotation)
Support education and research (AA School campus)
constraints: Financial: timber revenue must fund transition
Deer pressure: roe and fallow — browsing limits natural regen
Ash dieback: Fraxinus excelsior declining across site
Access: limited machinery access in steep combes
Campus: active educational use, public access paths
research_priorities: Drought-adapted oak/beech provenance for future climate
Mycorrhizal inoculation to accelerate broadleaf establishment
Continuous cover silviculture for conifer→broadleaf transition
Riparian corridor design for water quality + habitat
Syntropic planting trials in cleared conifer areas
Soil carbon monitoring under different management regimes
